sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **ConvertKit connector** integrates with ConvertKit via the REST API v3. It enables Shielva to sync subscribers, sequences, forms, tags, and broadcasts from your ConvertKit account into the knowledge base.

This connector uses **API Key** authentication — the key is sent as `?api_key=` query param on all GET requests. An optional API Secret is required for listing subscribers.

**Key capabilities:**
- Validate the API key via `GET /v3/account` on install and health check
- Sync all subscribers with page-based pagination (handles audiences of any size)
- Sync email sequences and opt-in forms
- Stable document IDs via SHA-256(prefix+resource_id)[:16] for deduplication across syncs
- Automatic retry with exponential back-off for transient errors and rate limits
- Circuit breaker (threshold: 5 failures, 60s recovery) for health degradation detection

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates api_key via GET /v3/account; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE with reason |
| `health_check()` | Calls GET /v3/account to verify key; returns account name in message |
| `sync()` | Fetches all subscribers (page-paginated) + sequences + forms; normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `list_subscribers(page, per_page)` | Page-based GET /v3/subscribers |
| `get_subscriber(id)` | GET /v3/subscribers/{id} for a single subscriber |
| `list_tags(page)` | GET /v3/tags — all tags |
| `list_sequences(page)` | GET /v3/sequences — email sequences |
| `list_forms(page)` | GET /v3/forms — opt-in forms |
| `list_broadcasts(page)` | GET /v3/broadcasts — one-off emails sent |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The connector uses **API Key** authentication.

- **Read endpoints (GET):** `?api_key={api_key}` query parameter
- **Subscriber listing:** `?api_secret={api_secret}` query parameter (elevated access required)

Both credentials are obtained from ConvertKit → Settings → Advanced → API.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|----------|-------------|
| API Key | `api_key` | Yes | Used for all read operations including health check |
| API Secret | `api_secret` | No | Required for listing subscribers |

sections.title: Sync Engine
sections.content:
The `sync()` method fetches three resource types in order:

1. **Subscribers** — page-based pagination via `?page=` + `per_page=1000`. Terminates when `total_subscribers` is reached.
2. **Sequences** — single request to `GET /v3/sequences`. Non-fatal if it fails.
3. **Forms** — single request to `GET /v3/forms`. Non-fatal if it fails.

Each resource is normalized to a `ConnectorDocument` with stable IDs before being pushed to the knowledge base.

children.title: Normalizers
children.content:
| Normalizer | Input | Stable ID formula | Document type |
|------------|-------|-------------------|---------------|
| `normalize_subscriber` | ConvertKit subscriber dict | `SHA-256("subscriber:" + str(id))[:16]` | subscriber |
| `normalize_sequence` | ConvertKit sequence dict | `SHA-256("sequence:" + str(id))[:16]` | sequence |
| `normalize_form` | ConvertKit form dict | `SHA-256("form:" + str(id))[:16]` | form |

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
All errors inherit from `ConvertKitError`. The exception hierarchy:

| Exception | HTTP Status | Description |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| `ConvertKitAuthError` | 401, 403 | Invalid or revoked API key |
| `ConvertKitNotFoundError` | 404 | Resource does not exist |
| `ConvertKitRateLimitError` | 429 | Rate limit exceeded; has `retry_after` attribute |
| `ConvertKitServerError` | 5xx | ConvertKit server-side error |
| `ConvertKitNetworkError` | — | Timeout or connection failure |

sections.title: Retry & Circuit Breaker
sections.content:
**Retry policy** (`with_retry`):
- Max 3 attempts
- Exponential back-off: `base_delay * 2^attempt + jitter(0–0.5s)`
- Max delay: 30s
- Auth errors are NOT retried
- Rate-limit errors honour the `Retry-After` header

**Circuit breaker**:
- Opens after 5 consecutive failures
- Recovery timeout: 60s
- State: closed → open → half-open → closed
